ogTitle: Eglinton vs Eighteen Mile: Climate Safety

heading: Eglinton vs Eighteen Mile — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Eglinton, New South Wales and Eighteen Mile, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Eglinton has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Eighteen Mile (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Eglinton or Eighteen Mile safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Eglinton has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Eighteen Mile scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Eglinton scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Eighteen Mile scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Eglinton or Eighteen Mile?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Eglinton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
